chinese_translation: 他/她/它/他们；也可作定冠词
example_french: vive la politique, vive l'amour
example_english: long live politics, long live love
replaces_what: replaces a direct object; le = masculine singular, la = feminine singular, les = plural
position_note: usually before the conjugated verb; before infinitive; after affirmative imperative
position_examples: Je le vois. | Je vais le voir. | Regarde-le !
usage_note: This seed row is mixed: det + pro. In a pronoun card, focus on object-pronoun use.
memory_note: As a pronoun, le/la/les replace a direct object. Do not confuse with le/la/les as articles before nouns.

example_french: je ne déteste pas cet homme
example_english: I do not detest this man
replaces_what: replaces or points to a thing, idea, or situation
position_note: often before être in expressions like c'est; can also appear as a determiner before a noun
position_examples: C'est vrai. | Ce sont mes amis. | Ce livre est intéressant.
usage_note: This seed row is mixed: det + pro. In pronoun use, ce often points to an idea/situation.
memory_note: Think of ce as a pointer word. As pronoun: c'est. As determiner: ce livre.

example_french: allez voir s'il est blessé
example_english: go see if he is injured
replaces_what: replaces a masculine singular subject; also used in impersonal structures
position_note: usually before the conjugated verb
position_examples: Il parle. | Il pleut. | Il est tard.
usage_note: Can refer to a masculine person/thing or act as an impersonal subject.
memory_note: Il can mean 'he/it', but in il pleut or il faut, it is just a grammar subject.

example_french: je ne sais pas à qui m'adresser
example_english: I don't know who to talk to
replaces_what: replaces a person or subject in a relative/interrogative structure
position_note: appears at the start of a question or relative clause
position_examples: Qui parle ? | La personne qui parle. | À qui parles-tu ?
usage_note: As relative pronoun, qui often acts as the subject of the relative clause.
memory_note: Qui often asks or links 'who'. In relative clauses, it usually carries the subject role.

example_french: avec ce traité, le Japon se rapproche des Etats-Unis
example_english: with this treaty, Japan brings itself closer to the U.S.
replaces_what: refers back to the subject in reflexive/pronominal verbs
position_note: before the conjugated verb; before infinitive in pronominal verbs
position_examples: Il se lave. | Ils se parlent. | Il va se lever.
usage_note: Used with pronominal verbs and reflexive meanings.
memory_note: Se means the action points back to the subject: il se lave = he washes himself.

example_french: je suis contente de vous revoir
example_english: I am happy to see you again
replaces_what: speaker as subject
position_note: before the conjugated verb; becomes j’ before vowel/silent h
position_examples: Je parle. | J'aime. | Je suis là.
usage_note: Subject pronoun for the speaker.
memory_note: Je becomes j’ before vowel sounds: j'aime, j'habite.

number_feature: singular verb form; meaning can be singular/plural
example_french: on tire et on pose les questions ensuite
example_english: we shoot first and ask questions later
replaces_what: indefinite subject; often used as informal nous
position_note: before the conjugated verb; takes 3rd person singular verb
position_examples: On parle français. | On y va ? | On est prêts.
usage_note: Very common in spoken French to mean 'we'. Verb stays singular.
memory_note: On feels plural sometimes, but grammar verb form is singular: on parle.

example_french: nous devons nous défendre nous-mêmes
example_english: we must defend ourselves
replaces_what: speaker + others as subject/object; also reflexive form
position_note: subject nous before verb; object/reflexive nous before verb
position_examples: Nous parlons. | Elle nous voit. | Nous nous lavons.
usage_note: Can be subject, object, or reflexive depending on position.
memory_note: Nous can appear twice in reflexive sentences: Nous nous défendons.

example_french: j'étais fou amoureux. elle m'aimait bien
example_english: I was head-over-heels in love. she loved me a lot
replaces_what: feminine singular subject; sometimes stressed pronoun
position_note: subject position before verb; stressed form after preposition or alone
position_examples: Elle parle. | Je pense à elle. | C'est elle.
usage_note: Can refer to a woman/girl or a feminine noun.
memory_note: Elle can mean she or it when replacing a feminine noun.

example_french: tu es celui que je respecte le plus
example_english: you're the one I respect the most
replaces_what: replaces a masculine singular noun already known from context
position_note: often followed by de or a relative clause
position_examples: Celui que je connais. | Celui de mon frère. | C'est celui-ci.
usage_note: Usually cannot stand alone without more context or a modifier.
memory_note: Celui needs a tail: celui que..., celui de..., celui-ci/là.

example_french: ici, vous avez une personnalité publique
example_english: here, you are a public personality
replaces_what: listener(s) as subject or object
position_note: subject vous before verb; object vous before verb
position_examples: Vous parlez. | Je vous écoute. | Asseyez-vous.
usage_note: Used for plural you or polite/formal singular you.
memory_note: Vous can be one polite person or several people. Verb form is always vous-form.

example_french: cela demande de l'intégrité et du courage
example_english: that requires integrity and courage
replaces_what: replaces a thing, idea, or situation
position_note: can be subject or object depending on sentence
position_examples: Cela demande du courage. | Je sais cela. | Cela dit...
usage_note: More formal than ça in many everyday contexts.
memory_note: Cela is like a formal 'that/it' for an idea or situation.

example_french: reviens me voir dans cinq ou six ans
example_english: come back to see me in five or six years
replaces_what: speaker as direct object, indirect object, or reflexive object
position_note: before the conjugated verb; becomes m’ before vowel/silent h
position_examples: Il me voit. | Il me parle. | Je m'appelle.
usage_note: Can be direct object, indirect object, or reflexive depending on verb.
memory_note: Me is not always 'me' in English word order; French places it before the verb.

example_french: mais j'ai confiance qu'en lui
example_english: but I have confidence in him
replaces_what: replaces à + person as indirect object; also stressed pronoun
position_note: indirect object before verb; stressed form after preposition or alone
position_examples: Je lui parle. | Je pense à lui. | C'est lui.
usage_note: As indirect object, lui can mean to him or to her.
memory_note: Lui is often for people with à: parler à Marie → lui parler.

chinese_translation: 其；关于/属于……的；de + 关系代词
example_french: on a tout ce dont tu rêvais en ville
example_english: everything you dreamed about is available in town
replaces_what: replaces de + noun/person in a relative clause
position_note: introduces a relative clause
position_examples: Ce dont tu parles. | La personne dont je parle. | Le livre dont j'ai besoin.
usage_note: Useful when the verb or expression uses de.
memory_note: Dont is the de-replacement relative pronoun: parler de → dont.

example_french: il y a bien des points sur lesquels je voudrais faire des commentaires
example_english: there are many points on which I would like to comment
replaces_what: replaces a specific noun, often after a preposition
position_note: often after prepositions; has agreement forms
position_examples: Lequel veux-tu ? | Sur lequel ? | Lesquels préfères-tu ?
usage_note: Forms include lequel, laquelle, lesquels, lesquelles.
memory_note: Lequel behaves like a pronoun with agreement. It often appears after prepositions.

example_french: a ma place, tu ne le ferais pas
example_english: in my position, you would not do it
replaces_what: one familiar listener as subject
position_note: before the conjugated verb
position_examples: Tu parles. | Tu viens ? | Tu le sais.
usage_note: Informal/familiar singular you.
memory_note: Tu is informal singular. Do not confuse with vous for formal or plural.

example_french: c'est eux qui comptent
example_english: they are the ones who matter
replaces_what: masculine or mixed plural people/things in stressed position
position_note: after preposition, after c'est, or used for emphasis
position_examples: C'est eux. | Je pense à eux. | Eux, ils savent.
usage_note: Stressed pronoun, not the normal subject form.
memory_note: Eux is the strong/stressed form. Subject form is ils.

example_french: je vais t'emmener chez un médecin
example_english: I'm going to take you to a doctor
replaces_what: familiar listener as object or reflexive object
position_note: before the conjugated verb; becomes t’ before vowel/silent h
position_examples: Je te vois. | Je te parle. | Tu t'appelles comment ?
usage_note: Informal singular object/reflexive pronoun.
memory_note: Te is before the verb in French: je te vois.

example_french: je ne sais pas de quoi vous parlez
example_english: I don't know what you're talking about
replaces_what: unknown thing or object of a preposition
position_note: often after a preposition or in informal questions
position_examples: De quoi parles-tu ? | Tu fais quoi ? | À quoi ça sert ?
usage_note: Often used after prepositions: de quoi, à quoi.
memory_note: Quoi often appears after prepositions; que is more often direct question object before verb.

example_french: ils ont chacun leurs intérêts, leurs passions
example_english: they each have their own interests, passions
replaces_what: each person/thing individually
position_note: can be subject or object; often used with chacun de
position_examples: Chacun a son rôle. | Chacun de nous. | Ils ont chacun leurs intérêts.
usage_note: Feminine form: chacune.
memory_note: Chacun means one by one, each individually.

example_french: il ne s'est pas sauvé lui-même
example_english: he didn't save himself
replaces_what: emphasizes that the masculine singular subject/object is itself
position_note: usually after the noun/pronoun it emphasizes or after the verb phrase
position_examples: Il l'a fait lui-même. | Lui-même le sait. | Il parle de lui-même.
usage_note: Feminine: elle-même; plural: eux-mêmes/elles-mêmes.
memory_note: The -même form adds emphasis: himself, by himself.

example_french: tout ceci est votre création
example_english: this is all your creation
replaces_what: points to a nearby thing, idea, or statement
position_note: can be subject or object
position_examples: Ceci est important. | Lis ceci. | Tout ceci est clair.
usage_note: Often more formal/written than ça.
memory_note: Ceci points to 'this here' or the thing being introduced.

gender_feature: usually masculine form; feminine quelqu'une is rare
example_french: il vaudrait mieux le demander au quelqu'un
example_english: it would be better to ask someone about it
replaces_what: an unspecified person
position_note: can be subject or object
position_examples: Quelqu'un arrive. | Je connais quelqu'un. | Demande à quelqu'un.
usage_note: Usually refers to a person, not a thing.
memory_note: Quelqu'un = some person. For things, use quelque chose.

example_french: la révolte n'est pas en elle-même un élément de civilisation
example_english: rebellion is not by itself an element of civilization
replaces_what: emphasizes that the feminine singular subject/object is itself
position_note: usually after the noun/pronoun it emphasizes or after the verb phrase
position_examples: Elle l'a fait elle-même. | Elle-même le sait. | En elle-même.
usage_note: Masculine: lui-même; plural: elles-mêmes/eux-mêmes.
memory_note: Elle-même adds emphasis: herself / itself.

example_french: j'aimerais redevenir moi-même
example_english: I would like to become myself again
replaces_what: emphasizes the speaker
position_note: usually after the noun/pronoun it emphasizes or after the verb phrase
position_examples: Je l'ai fait moi-même. | Moi-même, je ne sais pas. | Je veux redevenir moi-même.
usage_note: Used for emphasis or identity.
memory_note: Moi-même = myself, often with emphasis.

example_french: j'ai retrouvé quelques-uns de ces poèmes
example_english: I recovered some of these poems
replaces_what: a few people or things
position_note: can be subject or object; often followed by de + plural noun
position_examples: Quelques-uns sont partis. | J'en ai choisi quelques-uns. | Quelques-uns de ces poèmes.
usage_note: Feminine form: quelques-unes.
memory_note: Quelques-uns is pronoun form; quelques alone is usually determiner before noun.

example_french: je donnerais ma chemise à quiconque en aurait besoin
example_english: I would give my shirt to anyone who needed it
replaces_what: any person who / whoever
position_note: usually introduces or refers to a generic person
position_examples: Quiconque veut venir peut venir. | Je l'aiderai, quiconque il soit.
usage_note: More formal than n'importe qui.
memory_note: Quiconque is formal: whoever / anyone who.

example_french: on n'aurait pas fait mieux nous-mêmes
example_english: we wouldn't have done better ourselves
replaces_what: emphasizes the speaker group
position_note: usually after the noun/pronoun it emphasizes or after the verb phrase
position_examples: Nous l'avons fait nous-mêmes. | Nous-mêmes, nous savons. | On l'a fait nous-mêmes.
usage_note: Used for emphasis or by ourselves.
memory_note: Nous-mêmes = ourselves. It adds emphasis to nous/on.

example_french: vous ne pouvez pas les louer à autrui
example_english: you're not allowed to rent them to others
replaces_what: other people in general
position_note: often after prepositions or in formal expressions
position_examples: Respecter autrui. | Penser à autrui. | Le bien d'autrui.
usage_note: Formal/literary word for others/other people.
memory_note: Autrui is formal. Think 'other people' rather than a specific person.
